data.title: Entegris names Sukhi Nagesh as CFO
data.description:
• Entegris (NASDAQ: ENTG) on Thursday said it has appointed Sukhi Nagesh as CFO, effective May 18, 2026.
  • Nagesh joins from Nielsen, where he serves as head of corporate development and M&A.
  • He previously held senior roles at GlobalFoundries and Marvell Technology, among others.
  • He will succeed interim CFO Mike Sauer, who has held the role since March 1 and will continue as vice president and chief accounting officer.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trust3 AI, Dell partner on AI-ready data lakehouse infrastructure
data.description:
• Trust3 AI on Thursday said it has expanded its strategic collaboration with Dell Technologies (DELL) to deliver a secure, governed data lakehouse infrastructure for enterprise AI workloads.
  • The joint solution integrates Trust3 AI’s governance platform into Dell’s data lakehouse architecture, enabling organizations to run analytics and autonomous AI workloads across hybrid and on-premises environments.
  • The offering includes features such as automated sensitive data discovery, centralized policy enforcement, data masking and encryption, and AI auditing aimed at regulatory compliance.
  • The companies said the platform is designed for regulated industries, including financial services, healthcare, and the public sector, with the goal of supporting broader adoption of AI while maintaining data security and compliance.
  • DELL +0.33% premarket to  $206.34.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: T. Rowe Price trades higher as Q1 fees rise despite fall in AUM
data.description:
T. Rowe Price Group (TROW) was trading higher as Q1 investment advisory fees rose even as the Baltimore, Maryland-based asset manager saw a sequential decrease in AUM.

Shares were 0.79% higher at $101.26 during Thursday pre-market trading.

Adjusted diluted EPS of $2.52 rose 13.0% from the year-ago quarter and surpassed the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.35.

Net operating income increased to $680.5M from $596.3M.

Net revenue of $1.86B was up 5.3% year-over-year and above the consensus of $1.85B.

Revenue was boosted by a 5.3% year-over-year growth in investment advisory fees, which reached $1.68B from $1.60B a year ago.

Investment advisory fees represent about 90% of the company's revenue.

Assets under management fell to $1.71B at Q1-end from $1.76B at the end of the fourth quarter of 2025. The company saw net client outflows of $13.7B during the quarter.

Investors from outside the U.S. accounted for 8.6% of the asset manager's AUM as of March 31, 2026, compared to 8.8% at December 31, 2025.

Notably, the investment advisory annualized effective fee rate with performance-based fees fell to 38.6 basis points during the quarter from 39.2 bps in the previous quarter and 40.3 bps a year ago.

"With the recent volatility and broadening of markets, our active management approach positions us to take advantage of the opportunities this climate brings. Our teams are advancing innovative strategies, new vehicles, and compelling solutions to meet the evolving needs of clients," said CEO Rob Sharps.

data.title: Ranpak Holdings GAAP EPS of -$0.12 in-line, revenue of $101.2M beats by $9.61M
data.description:
• Ranpak Holdings press release (PACK): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.12 in-line.

data.title: Hubbell beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Hubbell press release (HUBB): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.93 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $1.52B (+10.9% Y/Y) beats by $20M.

  • Net cash provided by operating activities was $87 million in the first quarter of 2026 versus net cash provided by operating activities of $37 million in the 2025 period.

  • Free cash flow was $46 million in the first quarter of 2026 versus $11 million in the comparable period of 2025.

  • SUMMARY & OUTLOOK 

  • For the full year 2026, Hubbell anticipates total sales growth of 8-11% including organic sales growth of 6-9% (prior 7% -9%) vs. estimated growth of 8.27% Y/Y. Hubbell expects 2026 GAAP diluted earnings per share in the range of $17.45 to $18.00 and adjusted diluted earnings per share (“Adjusted EPS”) in the range of $19.30 to $19.85 vs. $19.75 consensus (prior $19.15-$19.85). For the full year, Adjusted EPS excludes amortization of acquisition-related intangible assets, which the Company expects to be approximately $1.70 per share, and transaction, integration, and separation costs, which the Company expects to be approximately $0.15 per share. The Company believes Adjusted EPS is a useful measure of underlying performance in light of our acquisition strategy.

    The diluted earnings per share and Adjusted EPS ranges are based on an adjusted tax rate of approximately 22.5% and include $15-20 million of anticipated restructuring and related investment. The Company continues to expect full year 2026 free cash flow conversion of 90% or greater on adjusted net income.
